Reason to Win: Fast compliant billing is the adoption gate for every counter shift and every future workflow.
Key Risk if Missing: Slow billing, invoice mistakes, and operator distrust can kill first-customer adoption.

Reason to Win: Reliable batch-first selling protects margin by reducing wrong-batch usage, expiry leakage, and stock confusion.
Key Risk if Missing: Negative stock, expiry leakage, and stock mismatch will break owner trust quickly.

Feature: Purchase import, inward, and receipt matching
Reason to Win: Large distributor invoices become practical only when inward is fast, validated, and receipt-aware.
Key Risk if Missing: Teams fall back to manual books or partial system usage, which corrupts stock truth from day one.

Reason to Win: This is one of the clearest margin-recovery loops in pharmacy and a strong owner-facing win theme.
Key Risk if Missing: Expiry and damaged stock turn into unrecovered losses, and supplier claims leak silently.

Reason to Win: It preserves sales when the requested brand is unavailable and gives PharmaFlow a smart-pharmacist counter story.
Key Risk if Missing: The store loses sales when unavailable brands cannot be replaced quickly with sellable alternatives.

Feature: Stock movement ledger, adjustments, and reconciliation
Reason to Win: Auditable stock movement is the backbone for trust across billing, inward, returns, and inspections.
Key Risk if Missing: Unexplained stock drift makes every report and every claim suspect.

Reason to Win: Actionable expiry handling turns alerts into money-saving decisions such as quarantine, transfer, dump, or RTV.
Key Risk if Missing: Stores see expiry risk but still react too late to prevent value loss.

Feature: Sales returns linked to original invoices
Reason to Win: A clean reverse sale flow keeps customer service, stock, and accounts aligned.
Key Risk if Missing: Return disputes and incorrect stock restoration create daily operational friction.

Reason to Win: Inspector-ready registers reduce legal risk and make the product credible for real pharmacy use.
Key Risk if Missing: Compliance failures can block rollout regardless of feature depth elsewhere.

Reason to Win: Searchable prescription evidence increases operational trust for repeat visits and restricted-medicine reviews.
Key Risk if Missing: The system looks incomplete in controlled-drug workflows and weak under inspection.

Feature: Audit trail, price-control, and override visibility
Reason to Win: Managers need to know who changed price, stock, or compliance-sensitive data and why.
Key Risk if Missing: Leakage, disputes, and abuse become hard to prove or correct.

Reason to Win: Practical reorder and shortage visibility protects sales by reducing preventable stockouts.
Key Risk if Missing: The store misses demand and loses credibility when common items go out of stock.

Feature: Multi-store roles, tenant scoping, and branch context
Reason to Win: Correct role and branch scoping keeps one-store rollout safe and preserves the architecture for multi-store expansion.
Key Risk if Missing: Cross-store visibility mistakes create security and commercial risk early.

Feature: Production trust layer: durable storage, monitoring, and rollback discipline
Reason to Win: A paid customer needs confidence that prescriptions, invoices, and deployments will not fail silently.
Key Risk if Missing: A technically good product can still fail commercially because the hosted setup is fragile.

Feature: HO / warehouse dashboard and operational visibility
Reason to Win: This becomes the control tower for shortages, expiries, claims, and receipts once multiple stores are live.
Key Risk if Missing: Multi-store rollout becomes founder-dependent because head office cannot see network risk clearly.

Feature: Inter-store transfer and warehouse replenishment orchestration
Reason to Win: It reduces urgent purchase leakage by moving available stock intelligently across the network.
Key Risk if Missing: Branches over-purchase while other branches sit on idle or near-expiry stock.

Reason to Win: A single claim cockpit is a high-signal owner feature because it shows recoverable money by supplier.
Key Risk if Missing: Claims remain scattered across notes, calls, and staff memory, so recoveries drop.

Reason to Win: Fast access to prior prescriptions, doctors, and restricted sales improves both safety and speed.
Key Risk if Missing: Repeat compliance-heavy sales stay slow and risky, especially for audit follow-up.

Reason to Win: These reports help owners decide what to buy less of, promote, transfer, or stop carrying.
Key Risk if Missing: The store operates reactively and cannot explain margin erosion or dead stock.

Reason to Win: If poor connectivity becomes real, resilient branch operation becomes a strong rollout differentiator.
Key Risk if Missing: Weak internet can block billing and receiving in the exact stores that need software reliability most.

Feature: Precomputed chain analytics and KPI history
Reason to Win: Large chains expect fast network-level analytics without waiting on raw transactional queries.
Key Risk if Missing: Reporting slows down as data grows, and HO dashboards stop scaling with the business.

Feature: Enterprise admin delegation and policy controls
Reason to Win: Large customers need cleaner delegation, policy templates, and safer admin boundaries.
Key Risk if Missing: As the chain grows, manual permission handling becomes messy and error-prone.

Reason to Win: This can help retention after trust-critical workflows are already stable.
Key Risk if Missing: It adds surface area without fixing the biggest first-customer pains.

Reason to Win: Useful follow-up communication can improve convenience once operations are stable.
Key Risk if Missing: Pursuing this too early distracts from core stock, purchase, return, and compliance trust.

Reason to Win: Delivery can expand service reach once store operations are dependable.
Key Risk if Missing: It adds operational complexity before the stock-and-returns engine is fully trusted.

Reason to Win: Integrations matter after the in-product workflows are clean enough to deserve automation.
Key Risk if Missing: Premature integrations create brittle scope and support burden before the core workflows are solid.
